prompt: A 19th-century scientific lithograph illustrating the concept of 'Gradient Descent'. The image should look like a genuine Victorian-era scientific diagram (1850s-1890s) with fine stippled line work, an aged parchment background, and a muted, hand-tinted watercolor palette of ochre, sepia, and verdigris. It depicts a three-dimensional topological contour map as if it were a geological cross-section specimen floating in the center of the page. Along the slopes of this landscape, a sequence of detailed, botanical-looking seed pods or small brass weights demonstrate iterative steps moving downward toward the global minimum at the bottom of the basin. The composition is highly structured, mimicking the layout of historical naturalist illustrations, with diagrammatic but organic elements. No modern computer graphics; entirely analog engraved appearance.
tradition_approach: Employed compositional arrangement of specimens floating on parchment, fine stippled line work characteristic of engravings, and a muted, hand-tinted watercolor palette (ochre, sepia, verdigris) over analog line art.
